behaviors.description: Generated function signature matches the interface required by the natural-language spec.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is not just local parsing logic; correctness depends on what code the model actually emits from the description. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess requirement beyond normal app calls. LLM-DEP: yes—the behavior is about model output content (parameter names/types/return type). MULTI-STEP: no, a single model decision can expose failure. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because behavior text is singular ('generated function signature matches...'), not a multi-step agent pipeline requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Run live generation for prompts with explicit signature constraints and assert parsed signature exactly matches expected parameter names, types, and return type.

behaviors.description: Orchestrator runs attempts sequentially and short-circuits on first passing attempt.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears at module handoff (orchestrator + generator client + test-result provider), not pure single-function algorithm only. REAL-INFRA: no concrete need for real OS/network/subprocess; in-process fakes can reproduce ordering/early-return bugs. LLM-DEP: no, this tests control flow, not model judgment. MULTI-STEP: no real agentic dependency required. INTEGRATION-BRAKE checks all negative, so Integration stands. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System/Agentic because behavior text is orchestration-only ('executes attempts in order and returns immediately').
behaviors.test_description: Wire orchestrator with deterministic fake generator and fake test executor (fail, then pass) and assert call order, attempt count, and immediate return after first pass.

behaviors.description: Live iterative loop can recover from an initial failure and pass within max attempts.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: stubs can force a pass but hide whether iterative refinement with real model behavior actually succeeds. REAL-INFRA: may involve real execution, but defining risk is agentic loop success. LLM-DEP: yes, outcome depends on real model outputs. MULTI-STEP: yes—generate, test feedback, refine, regenerate across attempts. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because behavior text explicitly requires iterative loop recovery ('iteratively refines... within allowed attempts'), i.e., multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real model on a task known to fail first attempt; run full loop and assert at least one later attempt passes before limit.

behaviors.description: Refinement prompt includes failed test names and failure messages after a failed attempt.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: primary risk is wiring/serialization from test results into prompt builder across modules. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required; synthetic failure payloads are sufficient. LLM-DEP: no, assertion is on constructed prompt content, not model quality. MULTI-STEP: no multi-agentic dependence needed to catch this bug. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because behavior text targets prompt construction ('prompt builder includes failed test names and failure messages'), which is deterministic.
behaviors.test_description: Run one failed attempt with a fake test executor returning named failures; assert next prompt payload contains each failed test name and message.

behaviors.description: Second live attempt changes code in response to prior failure context instead of resubmitting identical code.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: deterministic mocks can fake a changed output and miss true responsiveness to failure context. REAL-INFRA: not primarily OS/network-bound. LLM-DEP: yes—requires real model decision about how to modify implementation. MULTI-STEP: yes—at least two model generations linked by failure context handoff. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because behavior text is explicitly sequential ('next live attempt changes... in response to prior failure context').
behaviors.test_description: Run two-attempt live loop with real model and real failure feedback; assert attempt 2 code differs meaningfully from attempt 1 and reflects cited failing condition.

behaviors.description: Service halts after three failed attempts and returns structured error containing all attempt outcomes.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is in cross-module attempt bookkeeping and error assembly, not model quality. REAL-INFRA: can be reproduced with in-process fake executor/generator always failing; no real subprocess needed for this specific boundary. LLM-DEP: no. MULTI-STEP: looped control flow exists but does not require real agentic decisions. INTEGRATION-BRAKE checks do not force upgrade. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System/Agentic because behavior text is stop-condition + structured error contract.
behaviors.test_description: Configure deterministic failures for three attempts; assert orchestrator stops at attempt 3 and returns error object with all three code/results entries.

behaviors.description: Deterministic end-to-end wiring (generate call, test execution, bookkeeping) works with stubbed model outputs.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text explicitly says 'non-agentic' and 'deterministic stubbed model outputs', disqualifying Agentic/Workflow. MOCK-MISS: this is exactly module wiring, so Unit is often too low. REAL-INFRA: unless explicitly requiring real subprocess/OS effects, in-process wiring test is sufficient. ADJACENT tie-breaker Integration vs System: lower level chosen because no concrete behavior-tied real-OS/network failure is specified.
behaviors.test_description: Run service end-to-end with stub model responses and deterministic test executor outputs; assert generate->test->bookkeeping path and final status are correct.

behaviors.description: Live full loop solves a known hard function that fails on first attempt.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: stubs cannot verify true solve capability after iterative refinement. REAL-INFRA: execution may involve real runner, but decisive risk is multi-step agentic performance. LLM-DEP: yes—depends on actual model-generated code quality. MULTI-STEP: explicitly yes ('generate, test, refine, retest'). REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because behavior text requires full multi-step live loop over multiple model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Select a benchmark function known to fail first try; run live generate-test-refine-retest loop and assert eventual pass within three attempts.
